data.title: Clean Energy Technologies receives Nasdaq notice over delayed quarterly filing
data.description:
• Clean Energy Technologies (Nasdaq: CETY) on Friday said it received a notice from Nasdaq after failing to timely file its quarterly report on Form 10-Q for the quarter ended March 31, 2026.
  • The company said the notice, received on May 26, relates to non-compliance with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5250((c))(1), which requires listed companies to timely file periodic reports with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.
  • Clean Energy Technologies said the notice has no immediate effect on the listing or trading of its securities.
  • Under Nasdaq rules, the company has 60 calendar days to submit a plan to regain compliance.
  • If Nasdaq accepts the plan, it may grant an extension until November 16, 2026, to regain compliance.
  • The company said it is working to complete and file the quarterly report.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Gold gains as anticipated U.S.-Iran deal could ease inflation fears
data.description:
Gold prices extended gains Friday as the U.S. and Iran appear close to a 60-day agreement that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz, with President Trump saying he is meeting with advisors to make "a final determination."

Optimism over a ceasefire extension lifted oil prices and pushed the dollar and bond yields lower, boosting dollar-denominated gold, which pays no interest and is priced in the greenback.

"Gold is thus continuing to behave contrary to its status as a safe haven asset and is moving in the opposite direction to the price of oil," Carsten Fritsch of Commerzbank said in a note. "This is because the market now views Fed interest rate hikes as less likely, a development from which gold benefits as a non-interest-bearing asset."

Meanwhile, data this week showed U.S. core PCE inflation—the Federal Reserve's preferred gauge of monthly price increases—grew at a slower 3.3% pace in April but remained above the Fed's 2% target rate, driven ​by higher energy ⁠prices linked to the Middle East conflict.

On Friday, front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for June delivery jumped 1.3% to $4,560.50/oz, and front-month Comex June silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished flat at $75.616/oz.

For the month of May, gold fell 1.2% while silver gained 2.8%.

Gold has traded in a relatively narrow range since its initial drop at the start of the war, as traders weighed conflicting signals about progress toward a ceasefire; the metal is down ~14% since the end of February.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (DUST), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SGDM), (SGDJ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Celularity face Nasdaq listing rule breach after missing Q1 10-Q SEC filing
data.description:
• Celularity (CELU) announced on Friday that it received a Nasdaq notice for not filing its Q1 2026 Form 10-Q on time, leading to non-compliance with listing rules.
  • However, there is no immediate impact on its stock, as it will continue to trade on the Nasdaq under “CELU.”
  • The company has given a 60-day window until July 28 to submit a plan to regain compliance with filing requirements.
  • If Nasdaq accepts the plan, the firm may receive an extension of up to 180 days, with a final deadline around November 16, 2026, to fix the filing delay.
  • The company says it is actively working to file the delayed report and restore full compliance.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Stardust Solar completes first tranche of private placement, cuts offering size
data.description:
• Stardust Solar Energy (SUNXF) said it completed the first tranche of its non-brokered private placement, raising about C$486,561 through the issuance of 6.49M units at C$0.075 each.
  • The renewable energy company also reduced the maximum size of the offering to C$1.5M from C$3M.
  • Proceeds will be used to repay outstanding convertible debentures, advance its Zambia utility-scale energy project, and support working capital needs. Additional tranches are expected to close in the coming weeks, subject to regulatory approvals.

data.title: Gulf Resources receives Nasdaq notice over delayed quarterly report
data.description:
• Gulf Resources (NASDAQ: GURE) on Friday said it received a notice from Nasdaq over non-compliance with listing rules after failing to timely file its quarterly report on Form 10-Q for the period ended March 31, 2026.
  • The company said the notice, received on May 26, has no immediate effect on the listing of its securities on Nasdaq.
  • Gulf Resources said it had previously received a delinquency notice on April 23 related to its failure to timely file its annual report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2025, and remains delinquent on that filing.
  • The company said it must submit a compliance plan by June 22, 2026.
  • If Nasdaq accepts the plan, it may grant the company until October 12, 2026, to regain compliance.
  • Gulf Resources said it is working to complete both its Form 10-K and Form 10-Q filings.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cloudastructure receives Nasdaq non-compliance notice over delayed filing
data.description:
• Cloudastructure (CSAI) received a Nasdaq notice on May 26, 2026, for failing to timely file its Q1 2026 report.
  • The company is not currently in compliance with Nasdaq.
  • Cloudastructure has 60 days to submit a plan to regain compliance with Nasdaq requirements.
  • If Nasdaq accepts the plan, the company could receive an extension until November 16, 2026, to regain compliance.

data.title: OpenAI said to have held talks with Citigroup, JPMorgan for IPO preparations
data.description:
OpenAI (OPENAI) has discussed with Citigroup (C) (CITI:CA) and JPMorgan Chase (JPM) about their addition to the bank lineup for its initial public offering, people familiar with the matter told Bloomberg News.

The Microsoft-backed (MSFT) creator of ChatGPT is preparing to launch what could become the largest IPO in history. The company had worked with Goldman Sachs (GS) and Morgan Stanley (MS) on a draft IPO.

The offering could come later this year, and a confidential IPO filing is expected within weeks, the Bloomberg report published on Friday noted.

However, the talks may not necessarily end in Citigroup and JPMorgan joining Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley on the preparations, according to the people who asked not to be identified because the matter is private. Other investment banks could also work on the IPO.

Rival Anthropic PBC (ANTHRO) had also reportedly held early talks with Wall Street banks about leading roles in its IPO. Goldman Sachs (GS), JPMorgan Chase (JPM), and Morgan Stanley (MS) were among those expected to be considered.

SpaceX (SPCX) was also said to be assembling one of the largest underwriting syndicates in recent years for its upcoming IPO.

Morgan Stanley (MS), Goldman Sachs (GS), JPMorgan Chase (JPM), Bank of America (BAC), and Citigroup (C) were expected to serve as lead bookrunners, while another 16 banks were set to participate in supporting roles across geographies and investor segments.

data.title: General Electric continues rally to sixth straight session
data.description:
General Electric (GE) widened its gains to the sixth straight session on Thursday as it rose 0.92% to end at $323.76.

The energy major started on its upward trajectory on May 20 when it closed 5.22% higher after BNP Paribas expressed optimism over the firm’s defense business. It added nearly 7% in between May 20 and May 28. On a YTD basis, however, shares of the oil company have risen only 3% in stark comparison to an over 10% rise in the wider S&P 500 index.

Looking at Seeking Alpha’s quant rating, the stock has been rated a Hold with a score of 3.31 out of 5. GE has been rated an A+ for its profitability but an F for its valuation. Seeking Alpha analysts, on the other hand, were optimistic about the stock and recommended buying it. Wall Street analysts were even bullish with a Strong Buy call.

Earlier this week, Seeking Alpha analyst Mike Zaccardi issued a Sell call for the stock due to concerns over its premium valuations and underperformance against the S&P 500 index.

“Despite robust fundamentals and a $170B commercial services backlog, GE's technicals indicate bearish patterns and limited near-term upside,” Zaccardi said.

data.title: Allied Gold drops after report $4B Zijin deal faces opposition in China (update)
data.description:
Update 4:30pm: Adds Allied Gold comment.

Allied Gold (AAUC) (AAUC:CA) 3.7% after a report that the company's $4 billion sale to China's Zijin Mining (ZIJMY) is facing delays due to opposition from regulators in Beijing. The deal spread on Friday widened to $8.43, the most since the $44 a share deal was announced in January.

China's regulator, the National Development and Reform Commission, has questioned the premium Zijin is paying as well as the risks associated with Allied’s gold mine in Mali, according to a  Financial Times report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

A delay in China's regulatory approval has become a big issue, with some questioning if the deal will close as planned, according to the FT report. The original agreement had a deadline of Friday, which can be extended if both sides agree.

 “There is strong industrial and commercial logic for this transaction, and both parties continue to work diligently towards closing," an Allied spokesperson told the FT.

Allied Gold said in a separate statement on Friday that the deal agreement had been extended to July 29. In addition, the company said it won Investment Canada Act approval for the transaction as well as as clearance from the Regional Competition Authority of the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS) and the Competition and Consumer Commission of the Comm

Late last month, Allied Gold (AAUC) confirmed the normal course conduct of business at its operating mines in Mali amid reports of conflict in the country.

Zijin Mining (ZIJMF) (ZIJMY) agreed to buy Allied Gold (AAUC) for ~C$5.5B (US$4B) in cash in January.

data.title: 3 things to look out for on Monday
data.description:
Wall Street pushed higher on Friday and closed at record highs across all three major averages.

The blue chip Dow (DJI) ended on top by +0.7%, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) advanced +0.2%, and the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) moved up by +0.2%.

Now, here are three things for investors to watch on Monday:

  • Kicking off the new month, both the S&P Global Manufacturing PMI and the ISM Manufacturing PMI will provide fresh barometers on U.S. industrial activity. The May ISM headline reading is forecasted to come in around 53.3, pointing to continued expansion and up from 52.7 in the prior month. Following April's report, where the prices paid component surged to its highest level since the 2022 inflation shock, investors will be particularly focused on this sub-index to gauge whether supply chain strains and input costs are still rocketing.
  • Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is set to post second-quarter earnings after market close. Wall Street expects the Texas-based IT and AI-networking solutions company to deliver EPS of $0.53, implying a Y/Y rise of 39.5%, while revenue is expected to rise 28% Y/Y to $9.77B during the quarter.
  • The Group of Seven energy and finance ministers are due to discuss the turmoil in the Middle East.
